004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.marshaller;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.marshall.jcs.*;
020import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
021
022/**
023 * A pairing of a {@link JcsSerializer} and {@link JsonParser} into a single class with
024 * convenience to/of methods.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 *    Produces canonical JSON per <a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8785">RFC 8785</a>.
028 *    Parsing uses the standard {@link JsonParser} since JCS output is valid JSON.
029 * </p>
030 *
031 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
032 * <p class='bjava'>
033 *    <jc>// Using static convenience methods.</jc>
034 *    String <jv>s</jv> = Jcs.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>myBean</jv>);
035 *    MyBean <jv>b</jv> = Jcs.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>s</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
036 * </p>
037 * <p class='bjava'>
038 *    <jc>// Using instance.</jc>
039 *    Jcs <jv>jcs</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Jcs();
040 *    String <jv>s</jv> = <jv>jcs</jv>.of(<jv>myBean</jv>);
041 *    MyBean <jv>b</jv> = <jv>jcs</jv>.to(<jv>s</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
042 * </p>
043 *
044 * <h5 class='figure'>Example output (bean with name/age):</h5>
045 * <p class='bjson'>
046 *    {<jok>"age"</jok>:<jov>30</jov>,<jok>"name"</jok>:<jov>"Alice"</jov>}
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
050 *    <li class='note'>
051 *       {@link java.math.BigDecimal} and {@link java.math.BigInteger} values beyond IEEE 754 double
052 *       precision range will lose precision or throw during serialization.
053 *    <li class='note'>
054 *       {@link Double#NaN}, {@link Double#POSITIVE_INFINITY}, and {@link Double#NEGATIVE_INFINITY}
055 *       are not permitted and will throw during serialization.
056 * </ul>
057 *
058 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
059 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8785">RFC 8785 — JSON Canonicalization Scheme</a>
060 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Jcs">JCS topic</a>
061 *    <li class='jc'>{@link JcsSerializer}
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link JsonParser}
063 * </ul>
064 */
065public class Jcs extends CharMarshaller {
066
067   /** Default reusable instance. */
068   public static final Jcs DEFAULT = new Jcs();
069
070   /**
071    * Constructor.
072    *
073    * <p>
074    * Uses {@link JcsSerializer#DEFAULT} and {@link JsonParser#DEFAULT}.
075    */
076   public Jcs() {
077      this(JcsSerializer.DEFAULT, JsonParser.DEFAULT);
078   }
079
080   /**
081    * Constructor.
082    *
083    * @param s The serializer to use for serializing output.
084    * @param p The parser to use for parsing input.
085    */
086   public Jcs(JcsSerializer s, JsonParser p) {
087      super(s, p);
088   }
089}
